title: Container connection to multi network each with a static IP address
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I am trying to connect a Podman container to multiple macvlan networks each with a static IP address. However, the containers gets static IP address of the first network. The static IP address of the other networks are ignored. Instead, auto allocation happens from the subnet assigned to the macvlan network.

I am using Docker Compose to run the container and assign multiple networks. With Docker (even back 1.13 version of RHEL7 distro), this issue is not observed. All network interfaces inside container get static IP addresses as per specification.

```
# cat docker-compose.yml
...
    networks:
      tme-mas-02_ens160:
        ipv4_address: 10.80.228.110
      tme-mas-02_ens192:
        ipv4_address: 10.85.16.11
...
networks:
  tme-mas-02_ens160:
    external:
      name: ens160
  tme-mas-02_ens192:
    external:
      name: ens192
...

# podman network ls | grep macvlan
39093c071800  ens160      0.4.0       macvlan
22e496b0a5ff  ens192      0.4.0       macvlan
```

**Describe the results you received:**
In the example above, when the container is started, ens160 inside container gets the address as specified. However, ens192 gets 10.85.16.2. When container is restarted, ens192 gets 10.85.16.3.

**Describe the results you expected:**
ens160 = 10.80.228.110
ens192 = 10.85.16.11

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman -v
podman version 3.3.1
```

comments.body: Please try his with podman 4.0, this should work now
Thanks @Luap99 . Could you point to the PR that fixed the issue.
